question: Using FY2025 Connectivity segment figures, calculate YoY growth in revenue, operating income, and Segment Adjusted EBITDA. What do the differential growth rates imply about operating leverage within Connectivity?
facts: Connectivity segment revenue was $7,599M in FY2024
Connectivity segment revenue was $11,387M in FY2025
Connectivity segment revenue grew 49.85% YoY in FY2025
Connectivity segment operating income was $2,006M in FY2024
Connectivity segment operating income was $4,423M in FY2025
Connectivity segment operating income grew 120.49% YoY in FY2025
Connectivity segment Adjusted EBITDA was $3,849M in FY2024
Connectivity segment Adjusted EBITDA was $7,168M in FY2025
Connectivity segment Adjusted EBITDA grew 86.23% YoY in FY2025
Segment Adjusted EBITDA expanded roughly 1.73× as fast as revenue
Operating income grew about 2.42× the revenue growth rate
Operating margin rose from 26.40% to 38.84%
Segment Adjusted EBITDA margin rose from 50.65% to 62.95%
Connectivity is a highly operationally-levered segment
Connectivity's cost structure is dominated by fixed or semi-fixed costs
The segment generates disproportionate growth in EBITDA and even more disproportionate growth in operating income

question: Estimate implied monthly Starlink ARPU using Q1 2026 Connectivity revenue and disclosed subscriber counts. State all assumptions and identify what the filing’s subscriber and revenue definitions exclude from this ARPU estimate.
facts: The implied monthly Starlink ARPU for Q1 2026 is approximately $113.09
Connectivity revenue for the three months ended March 31, 2026 was $3,257 million
Starlink Subscribers as of December 31, 2025 were 8.9 million
Starlink Subscribers as of March 31, 2026 were 10.3 million
The average subscriber base during Q1 2026 is assumed to be 9.6 million, calculated as the simple average of beginning- and end-of-period balances
The quarter is assumed to be exactly three months
Connectivity revenue is assumed to be recognized evenly across the quarter with no intra-quarter seasonality
The 8.9 million and 10.3 million subscriber figures are assumed to be accurate as reported
If using only the end-of-period subscriber count of 10.3 million, the implied monthly ARPU would be $105.40
The filing's reported Starlink Subscriber ARPU is $66 per month for Q1 2026
The Starlink Subscribers metric counts unique Service Lines, not individual end users
Multiple people in a household sharing one Service Line are excluded from the Starlink Subscribers count
A single customer with multiple Service Lines is counted multiple times in the Starlink Subscribers count
Managed enterprise and government customers with contracts in aviation, maritime, land mobility, fixed sites, and government entities are excluded from the Starlink Subscribers count
The filing defines ARPU as service revenue generated from Starlink Subscribers only
Hardware sales of $380 million in Q1 2026 are excluded from the ARPU numerator
Enterprise & Government revenue of $1,109 million in Q1 2026 is excluded from the ARPU numerator
Consumer revenue was $2,148 million in Q1 2026
A portion of Consumer revenue representing hardware sales is excluded from the ARPU numerator
Removing Enterprise & Government revenue ($1,109 million) and Hardware revenue ($380 million) from the numerator yields an implied monthly ARPU of roughly $61.39

question: Calculate AI and Connectivity segment capex intensity for FY2025 and Q1 2026. Compare quarter versus full-year intensity and assess what the change implies about the pace and stage of AI infrastructure buildout.
facts: AI segment revenue was $3,201M in FY2025
AI segment capex was $12,727M in FY2025
AI segment revenue was $818M in Q1 2026
AI segment capex was $7,723M in Q1 2026
Connectivity segment revenue was $11,387M in FY2025
Connectivity segment capex was $4,178M in FY2025
Connectivity segment revenue was $3,257M in Q1 2026
Connectivity segment capex was $1,332M in Q1 2026
AI capex intensity was 3.98x (397.59%) in FY2025
AI capex intensity was 9.44x (944.13%) in Q1 2026
Connectivity capex intensity was 0.37x (36.69%) in FY2025
Connectivity capex intensity was 0.41x (40.90%) in Q1 2026
AI capex intensity increased by +5.46x from FY2025 to Q1 2026
AI capex intensity increased by 137.46% from FY2025 to Q1 2026
Connectivity capex intensity increased by +0.04x from FY2025 to Q1 2026
Connectivity capex intensity increased by 11.46% from FY2025 to Q1 2026
Annualized Q1 2026 AI capex would be roughly $30.9 billion versus $12.7 billion in FY2025
Annualized Q1 2026 AI revenue is roughly flat at ~$3.27 billion versus $3.20 billion in FY2025
Annualized Q1 2026 Connectivity capex would be ~$5.3 billion versus $4.2 billion in FY2025
Annualized Q1 2026 Connectivity revenue would be ~$13.0 billion versus $11.4 billion in FY2025
The AI segment is deploying nearly $9.50 of capital for every $1 of AI revenue generated in Q1 2026
The AI segment is in the deep infrastructure-deployment stage of its AI strategy
Capital is being committed to GPU clusters, data centers, and power generation while monetization is still ramping
There is a multi-year investment horizon before deployments translate into sustained positive AI Segment Adjusted EBITDA
Connectivity's stable ~0.4x intensity reflects a more mature segment where spending scales roughly in line with subscriber and revenue growth
The modest uptick in Connectivity Q1 2026 capex intensity suggests continued network densification but not a fundamental shift in investment stage
